item.title: Welcome to Sh1n Chronicles
item.description:
Welcome

This is where the deep dives happen. Research, forensics, threat intelligence, and everything in between.

Each post here explores a topic thoroughly — not quick takes, but actual investigation and learning.

What to expect

  • Forensics deep-dives: DFIR techniques, memory analysis, incident reconstruction
  • Threat Intelligence: Analyzing TTPs, malware campaigns, security trends
  • Detection Engineering: Sigma rules, logging strategies, detection approaches
  • Cryptography research: Challenges, solutions, algorithm breakdowns
  • Security tools & techniques: In-depth reviews and walkthroughs

Stay tuned.

item.title: Reflective PE Loaders: Same Trick, Five Disguises
item.description: Four droppers, one shellcode stager, and the same PEB-walking trick wearing different clothes. A side-by-side breakdown of what actually changes between samples — and what doesn’t.

item.title: ESC15 in the Wild: Walking an ADCS Template to Domain Admin
item.description: Abusing a vulnerable certificate template to escalate from a standard domain user to Domain Admin — and the exact log artifacts that would have caught it.

item.title: Havoc C2: Decrypting the Wire Format From a Cold PCAP
item.description: Recovering the full C2 traffic structure from a cold capture with no memory dump and no key on disk. The wire format, byte by byte.

item.title: Building NeuralDump: Designing a Box Around a Real Memory Leak
item.description: Notes from turning a real-world memory disclosure bug into a Proving Grounds machine — including the three exploit chains I built and threw away.

item.title: About
item.description:
Sh1n

DFIR analyst working across both sides of the fence — red team offense, blue team detection, and everything in between. Competes with CTF team Escadron. This blog is where the rest of it lives — the stuff that doesn’t fit in an incident report.

Clearances & Certifications

OSCP PJPT HTB CDSA HTB CWES

Focus Areas

  • DFIR & Forensics — Memory analysis, artifact recovery, timeline reconstruction
  • Malware Analysis — Reverse engineering, static/dynamic analysis, C2 decryption
  • Threat Intelligence — Campaign attribution, TTP tracking, indicator structuring
  • Detection Engineering — Sigma rules, correlation logic, SOC operations
  • Cryptography — CTF challenges, oracle attacks, algorithm breaking
  • Security Research — Zero-day analysis, vulnerability exploitation, tool development

About This Blog

Reverse-engineering what everyone else skips. Malware internals, live incident analysis, and CTF writeups that show the dead ends and the wrong turns — not just the flag.
